column4: And the LORD said unto {{c1::Moses}}: ‘Go in unto Pharaoh; for I have hardened his heart, and the heart of his servants, that I might show these My signs in the midst of them;
And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘{{c1::Go}} in unto Pharaoh; for I have hardened his heart, and the heart of his servants, that I might show these My signs in the midst of them;
and that thou mayest tell in the ears of thy son, and of thy son’s son, what {{c1::I}} have wrought upon Egypt, and My signs which I have done among them; that ye may know that I am the LORD.’
and that thou mayest tell in the ears of thy son, and of thy son’s son, what I have wrought upon {{c1::Egypt}}, and My signs which I have done among them; that ye may know that I am the LORD.’

And {{c1::Moses}} and Aaron went in unto Pharaoh, and said unto him: ‘Thus saith the LORD, the God of the Hebrews: How long wilt thou refuse to humble thyself before Me? let My people go, that they may serve Me.

And Moses and {{c1::Aaron}} went in unto Pharaoh, and said unto him: ‘Thus saith the LORD, the God of the Hebrews: How long wilt thou refuse to humble thyself before Me? let My people go, that they may serve Me.
Else, if thou refuse to let {{c1::My}} people go, behold, to-morrow will I bring locusts into thy border;
Else, if thou refuse to let My people go, behold, to-morrow will {{c1::I}} bring locusts into thy border;

and they shall cover the face of the earth, that one shall not be able to see the earth; and they shall eat the {{c1::residue}} of that which is escaped, which remaineth unto you from the hail, and shall eat every tree which groweth for you out of the field;

and they shall cover the face of the earth, that one shall not be able to see the earth; and they shall eat the residue of that which is {{c1::escaped}}, which remaineth unto you from the hail, and shall eat every tree which groweth for you out of the field;

and thy houses shall be filled, and the houses of all thy servants, and the houses of all the {{c1::Egyptians}}; as neither thy fathers nor thy fathers’fathers have seen, since the day that they were upon the earth unto this day.’ And he turned, and went out from Pharaoh.

and thy houses shall be filled, and the houses of all thy servants, and the houses of all the Egyptians; as neither thy fathers nor thy fathers’fathers have seen, since the day that they were upon the earth unto this day.’ And he turned, and went out from {{c1::Pharaoh}}.
And {{c1::Pharaoh’s}} servants said unto him: ‘How long shall this man be a snare unto us? let the men go, that they may serve the LORD their God, knowest thou not yet that Egypt is destroyed?’
And Pharaoh’s servants said unto him: ‘{{c1::How}} long shall this man be a snare unto us? let the men go, that they may serve the LORD their God, knowest thou not yet that Egypt is destroyed?’
And {{c1::Moses}} and Aaron were brought again unto Pharaoh; and he said unto them: ‘Go, serve the LORD your God; but who are they that shall go?’
And Moses and {{c1::Aaron}} were brought again unto Pharaoh; and he said unto them: ‘Go, serve the LORD your God; but who are they that shall go?’
And {{c1::Moses}} said: ‘We will go with our young and with our old, with our sons and with our daughters, with our flocks and with our herds we will go; for we must hold a feast unto the LORD.’
And Moses said: ‘{{c1::We}} will go with our young and with our old, with our sons and with our daughters, with our flocks and with our herds we will go; for we must hold a feast unto the LORD.’
And he said unto them: ‘{{c1::So}} be the LORD with you, as I will let you go, and your little ones; see ye that evil is before your face.
And he said unto them: ‘So be the LORD with you, as {{c1::I}} will let you go, and your little ones; see ye that evil is before your face.
Not so; go now ye that are men, and serve the LORD; for that is what ye desire.’ And they were driven out from {{c1::Pharaoh’s}} presence.
Not so; go now ye that are men, and serve the LORD; for that is what ye {{c1::desire}}.’ And they were driven out from Pharaoh’s presence.

And the LORD said unto {{c1::Moses}}: ‘Stretch out thy hand over the land of Egypt for the locusts, that they may come up upon the land of Egypt, and eat every herb of the land, even all that the hail hath left.’

And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘{{c1::Stretch}} out thy hand over the land of Egypt for the locusts, that they may come up upon the land of Egypt, and eat every herb of the land, even all that the hail hath left.’

And {{c1::Moses}} stretched forth his rod over the land of Egypt, and the LORD brought an east wind upon the land all that day, and all the night; and when it was morning, the east wind brought the locusts.

And Moses stretched forth his rod over the land of {{c1::Egypt}}, and the LORD brought an east wind upon the land all that day, and all the night; and when it was morning, the east wind brought the locusts.

And the locusts went up over all the land of {{c1::Egypt}}, and rested in all the borders of Egypt; very grievous were they; before them there were no such locusts as they, neither after them shall be such.

And the {{c1::locusts}} went up over all the land of Egypt, and rested in all the borders of Egypt; very grievous were they; before them there were no such locusts as they, neither after them shall be such.

For they covered the face of the whole earth, so that the land was darkened; and they did eat every herb of the land, and all the fruit of the trees which the hail had left; and there remained not any green thing, either tree or herb of the field, through all the land of {{c1::Egypt}}.

For they {{c1::covered}} the face of the whole earth, so that the land was darkened; and they did eat every herb of the land, and all the fruit of the trees which the hail had left; and there remained not any green thing, either tree or herb of the field, through all the land of Egypt.
Then {{c1::Pharaoh}} called for Moses and Aaron in haste; and he said: ‘I have sinned against the LORD your God, and against you.
Then Pharaoh called for {{c1::Moses}} and Aaron in haste; and he said: ‘I have sinned against the LORD your God, and against you.
Now therefore forgive, {{c1::I}} pray thee, my sin only this once, and entreat the LORD your God, that He may take away from me this death only.’
Now therefore forgive, I pray thee, my sin only this once, and entreat the LORD your God, that {{c1::He}} may take away from me this death only.’
And he went out from {{c1::Pharaoh}}, and entreated the LORD.
And he went out from Pharaoh, and {{c1::entreated}} the LORD.
And the LORD turned an exceeding strong west wind, which took up the locusts, and drove them into the {{c1::Red}} Sea; there remained not one locust in all the border of Egypt.
And the LORD turned an exceeding strong west wind, which took up the locusts, and drove them into the Red {{c1::Sea}}; there remained not one locust in all the border of Egypt.
But the LORD hardened {{c1::Pharaoh’s}} heart, and he did not let the children of Israel go.
But the LORD hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he did not let the children of {{c1::Israel}} go.
And the LORD said unto {{c1::Moses}}: ‘Stretch out thy hand toward heaven, that there may be darkness over the land of Egypt, even darkness which may be felt.’
And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘{{c1::Stretch}} out thy hand toward heaven, that there may be darkness over the land of Egypt, even darkness which may be felt.’
And {{c1::Moses}} stretched forth his hand toward heaven; and there was a thick darkness in all the land of Egypt three days;
And Moses stretched forth his hand toward heaven; and there was a thick darkness in all the land of {{c1::Egypt}} three days;
they saw not one another, neither rose any from his place for three days; but all the children of {{c1::Israel}} had light in their dwellings.
they saw not one {{c1::another}}, neither rose any from his place for three days; but all the children of Israel had light in their dwellings.
And {{c1::Pharaoh}} called unto Moses, and said: ‘Go ye, serve the LORD; only let your flocks and your herds be stayed; let your little ones also go with you.’
And Pharaoh called unto {{c1::Moses}}, and said: ‘Go ye, serve the LORD; only let your flocks and your herds be stayed; let your little ones also go with you.’
And {{c1::Moses}} said: ‘Thou must also give into our hand sacrifices and burnt-offerings, that we may sacrifice unto the LORD our God.
And Moses said: ‘{{c1::Thou}} must also give into our hand sacrifices and burnt-offerings, that we may sacrifice unto the LORD our God.

Our {{c1::cattle}} also shall go with us; there shall not a hoof be left behind; for thereof must we take to serve the LORD our God; and we know not with what we must serve the LORD, until we come thither.’

Our cattle also shall go with us; there shall not a hoof be left {{c1::behind}}; for thereof must we take to serve the LORD our God; and we know not with what we must serve the LORD, until we come thither.’
But the LORD hardened {{c1::Pharaoh’s}} heart, and he would not let them go.
But the LORD {{c1::hardened}} Pharaoh’s heart, and he would not let them go.
And {{c1::Pharaoh}} said unto him: ‘Get thee from me, take heed to thyself, see my face no more; for in the day thou seest my face thou shalt die.’
And Pharaoh said unto him: ‘{{c1::Get}} thee from me, take heed to thyself, see my face no more; for in the day thou seest my face thou shalt die.’
And {{c1::Moses}} said: ‘Thou hast spoken well; I will see thy face again no more.’
And Moses said: ‘{{c1::Thou}} hast spoken well; I will see thy face again no more.’

And the LORD said unto {{c1::Moses}}: ‘Yet one plague more will I bring upon Pharaoh, and upon Egypt; afterwards he will let you go hence; when he shall let you go, he shall surely thrust you out hence altogether.

And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘{{c1::Yet}} one plague more will I bring upon Pharaoh, and upon Egypt; afterwards he will let you go hence; when he shall let you go, he shall surely thrust you out hence altogether.
Speak now in the ears of the {{c1::people}}, and let them ask every man of his neighbour, and every woman of her neighbour, jewels of silver, and jewels of gold.’
Speak now in the ears of the people, and let them ask every man of his {{c1::neighbour}}, and every woman of her neighbour, jewels of silver, and jewels of gold.’

And the LORD gave the people favour in the sight of the {{c1::Egyptians}}. Moreover the man Moses was very great in the land of Egypt, in the sight of Pharaoh’s servants, and in the sight of the people.

And the LORD gave the people favour in the sight of the Egyptians. {{c1::Moreover}} the man Moses was very great in the land of Egypt, in the sight of Pharaoh’s servants, and in the sight of the people.
And {{c1::Moses}} said: ‘Thus saith the LORD: About midnight will I go out into the midst of Egypt;
And Moses said: ‘{{c1::Thus}} saith the LORD: About midnight will I go out into the midst of Egypt;

and all the first-born in the land of {{c1::Egypt}} shall die, from the first-born of Pharaoh that sitteth upon his throne, even unto the first-born of the maid-servant that is behind the mill; and all the first-born of cattle.

and all the first-born in the land of Egypt shall die, from the first-born of {{c1::Pharaoh}} that sitteth upon his throne, even unto the first-born of the maid-servant that is behind the mill; and all the first-born of cattle.
And there shall be a great cry throughout all the land of {{c1::Egypt}}, such as there hath been none like it, nor shall be like it any more.
And there shall be a great cry {{c1::throughout}} all the land of Egypt, such as there hath been none like it, nor shall be like it any more.
But against any of the children of {{c1::Israel}} shall not a dog whet his tongue, against man or beast; that ye may know how that the LORD doth put a difference between the Egyptians and Israel.
But against any of the children of Israel shall not a dog whet his tongue, against man or beast; that ye may know how that the LORD doth put a difference between the {{c1::Egyptians}} and Israel.

And all these thy servants shall come down unto me, and bow down unto me, saying: {{c1::Get}} thee out, and all the people that follow thee; and after that I will go out.’ And he went out from Pharaoh in hot anger.

And all these thy servants shall come down unto me, and bow down unto me, saying: Get thee out, and all the people that follow thee; and after that {{c1::I}} will go out.’ And he went out from Pharaoh in hot anger.
And the LORD said unto {{c1::Moses}}: ‘Pharaoh will not hearken unto you; that My wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.’
And the LORD said unto Moses: ‘{{c1::Pharaoh}} will not hearken unto you; that My wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.’
And {{c1::Moses}} and Aaron did all these wonders before Pharaoh; and the LORD hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he did not let the children of Israel go out of his land.
And Moses and {{c1::Aaron}} did all these wonders before Pharaoh; and the LORD hardened Pharaoh’s heart, and he did not let the children of Israel go out of his land.
And the LORD spoke unto {{c1::Moses}} and Aaron in the land of Egypt, saying:
And the LORD spoke unto Moses and {{c1::Aaron}} in the land of Egypt, saying:
’This month shall be unto you the {{c1::beginning}} of months; it shall be the first month of the year to you.
’This month shall be unto you the beginning of {{c1::months}}; it shall be the first month of the year to you.
Speak ye unto all the congregation of {{c1::Israel}}, saying: In the tenth day of this month they shall take to them every man a lamb, according to their fathers’houses, a lamb for a household;
Speak ye unto all the {{c1::congregation}} of Israel, saying: In the tenth day of this month they shall take to them every man a lamb, according to their fathers’houses, a lamb for a household;

and if the {{c1::household}} be too little for a lamb, then shall he and his neighbour next unto his house take one according to the number of the souls; according to every man’s eating ye shall make your count for the lamb.

and if the household be too {{c1::little}} for a lamb, then shall he and his neighbour next unto his house take one according to the number of the souls; according to every man’s eating ye shall make your count for the lamb.
Your lamb shall be {{c1::without}} blemish, a male of the first year; ye shall take it from the sheep, or from the goats;
Your lamb shall be without {{c1::blemish}}, a male of the first year; ye shall take it from the sheep, or from the goats;
and ye shall keep it unto the fourteenth day of the same month; and the whole assembly of the congregation of {{c1::Israel}} shall kill it at dusk.
and ye shall keep it unto the {{c1::fourteenth}} day of the same month; and the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel shall kill it at dusk.
And they shall take of the blood, and put it on the two side-posts and on the {{c1::lintel}}, upon the houses wherein they shall eat it.
And they shall take of the blood, and put it on the two side-posts and on the lintel, upon the {{c1::houses}} wherein they shall eat it.
And they shall eat the flesh in that night, roast with fire, and {{c1::unleavened}} bread; with bitter herbs they shall eat it.
And they shall eat the flesh in that night, roast with fire, and unleavened bread; with {{c1::bitter}} herbs they shall eat it.
Eat not of it raw, nor {{c1::sodden}} at all with water, but roast with fire; its head with its legs and with the inwards thereof.
Eat not of it raw, nor sodden at all with water, but roast with fire; its head with its legs and with the {{c1::inwards}} thereof.
And ye shall let {{c1::nothing}} of it remain until the morning; but that which remaineth of it until the morning ye shall burn with fire.
And ye shall let nothing of it {{c1::remain}} until the morning; but that which remaineth of it until the morning ye shall burn with fire.
And thus shall ye eat it: with your loins girded, your shoes on your feet, and your staff in your hand; and ye shall eat it in haste—it is the {{c1::LORD’s}} passover.
And thus shall ye eat it: with your loins {{c1::girded}}, your shoes on your feet, and your staff in your hand; and ye shall eat it in haste—it is the LORD’s passover.

For {{c1::I}} will go through the land of Egypt in that night, and will smite all the first-born in the land of Egypt, both man and beast; and against all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgments: I am the LORD.

For I will go through the land of {{c1::Egypt}} in that night, and will smite all the first-born in the land of Egypt, both man and beast; and against all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgments: I am the LORD.

And the blood shall be to you for a token upon the houses where ye are; and when {{c1::I}} see the blood, I will pass over you, and there shall no plague be upon you to destroy you, when I smite the land of Egypt.

And the blood shall be to you for a token upon the houses where ye are; and when I see the blood, I will pass over you, and there shall no plague be upon you to destroy you, when I smite the land of {{c1::Egypt}}.
And this day shall be unto you for a {{c1::memorial}}, and ye shall keep it a feast to the LORD; throughout your generations ye shall keep it a feast by an ordinance for ever.
And this day shall be unto you for a memorial, and ye shall keep it a feast to the LORD; {{c1::throughout}} your generations ye shall keep it a feast by an ordinance for ever.

Seven days shall ye eat unleavened bread; howbeit the first day ye shall put away leaven out of your houses; for whosoever eateth leavened bread from the first day until the seventh day, that soul shall be cut off from {{c1::Israel}}.

Seven days shall ye eat {{c1::unleavened}} bread; howbeit the first day ye shall put away leaven out of your houses; for whosoever eateth leavened bread from the first day until the seventh day, that soul shall be cut off from Israel.

And in the first day there shall be to you a holy {{c1::convocation}}, and in the seventh day a holy convocation; no manner of work shall be done in them, save that which every man must eat, that only may be done by you.

And in the first day there shall be to you a holy convocation, and in the {{c1::seventh}} day a holy convocation; no manner of work shall be done in them, save that which every man must eat, that only may be done by you.

And ye shall observe the feast of unleavened bread; for in this selfsame day have {{c1::I}} brought your hosts out of the land of Egypt; therefore shall ye observe this day throughout your generations by an ordinance for ever.

And ye shall observe the feast of unleavened bread; for in this selfsame day have I brought your hosts out of the land of {{c1::Egypt}}; therefore shall ye observe this day throughout your generations by an ordinance for ever.
In the first month, on the {{c1::fourteenth}} day of the month at even, ye shall eat unleavened bread, until the one and twentieth day of the month at even.
In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at even, ye shall eat {{c1::unleavened}} bread, until the one and twentieth day of the month at even.

Seven days shall there be no leaven found in your houses; for whosoever eateth that which is leavened, that soul shall be cut off from the congregation of {{c1::Israel}}, whether he be a sojourner, or one that is born in the land.

Seven days shall there be no {{c1::leaven}} found in your houses; for whosoever eateth that which is leavened, that soul shall be cut off from the congregation of Israel, whether he be a sojourner, or one that is born in the land.
Ye shall eat {{c1::nothing}} leavened; in all your habitations shall ye eat unleavened bread.’
Ye shall eat nothing {{c1::leavened}}; in all your habitations shall ye eat unleavened bread.’
Then {{c1::Moses}} called for all the elders of Israel, and said unto them: ‘Draw out, and take you lambs according to your families, and kill the passover lamb.
Then Moses called for all the elders of {{c1::Israel}}, and said unto them: ‘Draw out, and take you lambs according to your families, and kill the passover lamb.

And ye shall take a bunch of {{c1::hyssop}}, and dip it in the blood that is in the basin, and strike the lintel and the two side-posts with the blood that is in the basin; and none of you shall go out of the door of his house until the morning.

And ye shall take a bunch of hyssop, and dip it in the blood that is in the basin, and {{c1::strike}} the lintel and the two side-posts with the blood that is in the basin; and none of you shall go out of the door of his house until the morning.

For the LORD will pass through to smite the {{c1::Egyptians}}; and when He seeth the blood upon the lintel, and on the two side-posts, the LORD will pass over the door, and will not suffer the destroyer to come in unto your houses to smite you.

For the LORD will pass through to smite the Egyptians; and when {{c1::He}} seeth the blood upon the lintel, and on the two side-posts, the LORD will pass over the door, and will not suffer the destroyer to come in unto your houses to smite you.
And ye shall {{c1::observe}} this thing for an ordinance to thee and to thy sons for ever.
And ye shall observe this thing for an {{c1::ordinance}} to thee and to thy sons for ever.
And it shall come to pass, when ye be come to the land which the LORD will give you, according as {{c1::He}} hath promised, that ye shall keep this service.
And it shall come to pass, when ye be come to the land which the LORD will give you, {{c1::according}} as He hath promised, that ye shall keep this service.
And it shall come to pass, when your children shall say unto you: {{c1::What}} mean ye by this service?
And it shall come to pass, when your {{c1::children}} shall say unto you: What mean ye by this service?

that ye shall say: {{c1::It}} is the sacrifice of the LORD’s passover, for that He passed over the houses of the children of Israel in Egypt, when He smote the Egyptians, and delivered our houses.’ And the people bowed the head and worshipped.

that ye shall say: It is the sacrifice of the {{c1::LORD’s}} passover, for that He passed over the houses of the children of Israel in Egypt, when He smote the Egyptians, and delivered our houses.’ And the people bowed the head and worshipped.
And the children of {{c1::Israel}} went and did so; as the LORD had commanded Moses and Aaron, so did they.
And the children of Israel went and did so; as the LORD had commanded {{c1::Moses}} and Aaron, so did they.

And it came to pass at midnight, that the LORD smote all the firstborn in the land of {{c1::Egypt}}, from the first-born of Pharaoh that sat on his throne unto the first-born of the captive that was in the dungeon; and all the first-born of cattle.

And it came to pass at midnight, that the LORD smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the first-born of {{c1::Pharaoh}} that sat on his throne unto the first-born of the captive that was in the dungeon; and all the first-born of cattle.
And {{c1::Pharaoh}} rose up in the night, he, and all his servants, and all the Egyptians; and there was a great cry in Egypt; for there was not a house where there was not one dead.
And Pharaoh rose up in the night, he, and all his servants, and all the {{c1::Egyptians}}; and there was a great cry in Egypt; for there was not a house where there was not one dead.
And he called for {{c1::Moses}} and Aaron by night and said: ‘Rise up, get you forth from among my people, both ye and the children of Israel; and go, serve the LORD, as ye have said.
And he called for Moses and {{c1::Aaron}} by night and said: ‘Rise up, get you forth from among my people, both ye and the children of Israel; and go, serve the LORD, as ye have said.
Take both your {{c1::flocks}} and your herds, as ye have said, and be gone; and bless me also.’
And the {{c1::Egyptians}} were urgent upon the people, to send them out of the land in haste; for they said: ‘We are all dead men.’
And the Egyptians were urgent upon the people, to send them out of the land in haste; for they said: ‘{{c1::We}} are all dead men.’
And the {{c1::people}} took their dough before it was leavened, their kneading-troughs being bound up in their clothes upon their shoulders.
And the people took their dough {{c1::before}} it was leavened, their kneading-troughs being bound up in their clothes upon their shoulders.
And the children of {{c1::Israel}} did according to the word of Moses; and they asked of the Egyptians jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and raiment.
And the children of Israel did according to the word of {{c1::Moses}}; and they asked of the Egyptians jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and raiment.
And the LORD gave the people favour in the sight of the {{c1::Egyptians}}, so that they let them have what they asked. And they despoiled the Egyptians.
And the LORD gave the {{c1::people}} favour in the sight of the Egyptians, so that they let them have what they asked. And they despoiled the Egyptians.
And the children of {{c1::Israel}} journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand men on foot, beside children.
And the children of Israel journeyed from {{c1::Rameses}} to Succoth, about six hundred thousand men on foot, beside children.
And a mixed {{c1::multitude}} went up also with them; and flocks, and herds, even very much cattle.
And a mixed multitude went up also with them; and {{c1::flocks}}, and herds, even very much cattle.

And they baked unleavened cakes of the dough which they brought forth out of {{c1::Egypt}}, for it was not leavened; because they were thrust out of Egypt, and could not tarry, neither had they prepared for themselves any victual.

And they baked {{c1::unleavened}} cakes of the dough which they brought forth out of Egypt, for it was not leavened; because they were thrust out of Egypt, and could not tarry, neither had they prepared for themselves any victual.
Now the time that the children of {{c1::Israel}} dwelt in Egypt was four hundred and thirty years.
Now the time that the children of Israel dwelt in {{c1::Egypt}} was four hundred and thirty years.
And it came to pass at the end of four hundred and thirty years, even the selfsame day it came to pass, that all the host of the LORD went out from the land of {{c1::Egypt}}.
And it came to pass at the end of four {{c1::hundred}} and thirty years, even the selfsame day it came to pass, that all the host of the LORD went out from the land of Egypt.

It was a night of watching unto the LORD for bringing them out from the land of {{c1::Egypt}}; this same night is a night of watching unto the LORD for all the children of Israel throughout their generations.

It was a night of watching unto the LORD for bringing them out from the land of Egypt; this same night is a night of watching unto the LORD for all the children of {{c1::Israel}} throughout their generations.
And the LORD said unto {{c1::Moses}} and Aaron: ‘This is the ordinance of the passover: there shall no alien eat thereof;
And the LORD said unto Moses and {{c1::Aaron}}: ‘This is the ordinance of the passover: there shall no alien eat thereof;
but every man’s {{c1::servant}} that is bought for money, when thou hast circumcised him, then shall he eat thereof.
but every man’s servant that is {{c1::bought}} for money, when thou hast circumcised him, then shall he eat thereof.
A {{c1::sojourner}} and a hired servant shall not eat thereof.
A sojourner and a hired {{c1::servant}} shall not eat thereof.
In one house shall it be eaten; thou shalt not carry forth aught of the flesh {{c1::abroad}} out of the house; neither shall ye break a bone thereof.
In one house shall it be eaten; thou shalt not carry forth aught of the flesh abroad out of the house; {{c1::neither}} shall ye break a bone thereof.
All the congregation of {{c1::Israel}} shall keep it.
All the {{c1::congregation}} of Israel shall keep it.

And when a {{c1::stranger}} shall sojourn with thee, and will keep the passover to the LORD, let all his males be circumcised, and then let him come near and keep it; and he shall be as one that is born in the land; but no uncircumcised person shall eat thereof.

And when a stranger shall {{c1::sojourn}} with thee, and will keep the passover to the LORD, let all his males be circumcised, and then let him come near and keep it; and he shall be as one that is born in the land; but no uncircumcised person shall eat thereof.
One law shall be to him that is {{c1::homeborn}}, and unto the stranger that sojourneth among you.’
One law shall be to him that is homeborn, and unto the {{c1::stranger}} that sojourneth among you.’
Thus did all the children of {{c1::Israel}}; as the LORD commanded Moses and Aaron, so did they.
Thus did all the children of Israel; as the LORD commanded {{c1::Moses}} and Aaron, so did they.
And it came to pass the selfsame day that the LORD did bring the children of {{c1::Israel}} out of the land of Egypt by their hosts.
And it came to pass the selfsame day that the LORD did bring the children of Israel out of the land of {{c1::Egypt}} by their hosts.
And the LORD spoke unto {{c1::Moses}}, saying:
And the LORD spoke unto Moses, {{c1::saying}}:
’Sanctify unto {{c1::Me}} all the first-born, whatsoever opens the womb among the children of Israel, both of man and of beast, it is Mine.’
’Sanctify unto Me all the first-born, whatsoever opens the womb among the children of {{c1::Israel}}, both of man and of beast, it is Mine.’

And {{c1::Moses}} said unto the people: ‘Remember this day, in which ye came out from Egypt, out of the house of bondage; for by strength of hand the LORD brought you out from this place; there shall no leavened bread be eaten.

And Moses said unto the people: ‘{{c1::Remember}} this day, in which ye came out from Egypt, out of the house of bondage; for by strength of hand the LORD brought you out from this place; there shall no leavened bread be eaten.
This day ye go forth in the month {{c1::Abib}}.

And it shall be when the LORD shall bring thee into the land of the {{c1::Canaanite}}, and the Hittite, and the Amorite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite, which He swore unto thy fathers to give thee, a land flowing with milk and honey, that thou shalt keep this service in this month.

And it shall be when the LORD shall bring thee into the land of the Canaanite, and the {{c1::Hittite}}, and the Amorite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite, which He swore unto thy fathers to give thee, a land flowing with milk and honey, that thou shalt keep this service in this month.
Seven days thou shalt eat {{c1::unleavened}} bread, and in the seventh day shall be a feast to the LORD.
Seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread, and in the {{c1::seventh}} day shall be a feast to the LORD.
{{c1::Unleavened}} bread shall be eaten throughout the seven days; and there shall no leavened bread be seen with thee, neither shall there be leaven seen with thee, in all thy borders.
Unleavened bread shall be eaten {{c1::throughout}} the seven days; and there shall no leavened bread be seen with thee, neither shall there be leaven seen with thee, in all thy borders.
And thou shalt tell thy son in that day, saying: {{c1::It}} is because of that which the LORD did for me when I came forth out of Egypt.
And thou shalt tell thy son in that day, saying: It is because of that which the LORD did for me when {{c1::I}} came forth out of Egypt.

And it shall be for a sign unto thee upon thy hand, and for a memorial between thine eyes, that the law of the LORD may be in thy mouth; for with a strong hand hath the LORD brought thee out of {{c1::Egypt}}.

And it shall be for a sign unto thee upon thy hand, and for a {{c1::memorial}} between thine eyes, that the law of the LORD may be in thy mouth; for with a strong hand hath the LORD brought thee out of Egypt.
Thou shalt {{c1::therefore}} keep this ordinance in its season from year to year.

that thou shalt set apart unto the LORD all that openeth the womb; every firstling that is a male, which thou hast coming of a beast, shall be the {{c1::LORD’s}}.

And every {{c1::firstling}} of an ass thou shalt redeem with a lamb; and if thou wilt not redeem it, then thou shalt break its neck; and all the first-born of man among thy sons shalt thou redeem.

and it came to pass, when {{c1::Pharaoh}} would hardly let us go that the LORD slew all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both the first-born of man, and the first-born of beast; therefore I sacrifice to the LORD all that openeth the womb, being males; but all the first-born of my sons I redeem.
